flags.concern: clip-resize-commit-plumbing: Step 3's default 'call onActionResizeEnd twice from TimelineCanvas' path is technically incomplete. In this repo the resize commit logic in useClipResize depends on a per-clip onActionResizeStart record and per-clip transaction id; the adjacent clip would never get either, so its from/to/speed math falls back to the already-resized action and the second commit loses transaction coalescing.
flags.evidence: Reworked Step 3 to fix the flagged correctness bug. Previously the plan defaulted to calling `onActionResizeEnd` twice from `TimelineCanvas`, but `useClipResize` keeps per-clip `resizeStartRef` (from/to/speed origin) and `resizeTransactionIdRef`, so the neighbor would never get a recorded origin and meta math would be wrong, plus the undo step would split. New plan extends both `onActionResizeStart` and `onActionResizeEnd` in `useClipResize.ts` to accept an optional `adjacent` clip, records both clips' origins on start under one shared transaction id, and commits both via a single `applyEdit` on end. Step 4 (TimelineCanvas) now resolves the neighbor at `handleResizePointerDown`, fires the start/end callbacks once each with `adjacent` populated, and skips `snapResize` only when an interior group neighbor is engaged. Added Step 3 as a dedicated hook-extension step (was previously folded into a paragraph in old Step 3). Updated test expectations in Step 5 to match the single-callback contract. Removed the resolved transaction-batching question. Promoted the single-undo-step criterion from `should` to `must`. Added a `must` for the type widening reaching all consumers (`useTimelineState.types.ts`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`).

flags.concern: resize-verification: Step 4's resize coverage is too shallow for the actual risk surface. The plan only proposes TimelineCanvas spy assertions on callback payloads, but the behavior that can break here lives in useClipResize's committed rows/meta/history updates, so the suite could still pass while the adjacent clip persists incorrect trim/speed data.
flags.evidence: The current TimelineCanvas tests already stop at callback assertions (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx:148). The real persistence logic is downstream in useClipResize, which rebuilds rows, resolves overlaps, and patches clip meta during onActionResizeEnd (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipResize.ts:122). The plan's new tests stay at the canvas boundary and would not catch a broken adjacent-clip commit path.
